page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-4 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled at 2 threads per core. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include bundled local storage or GPU hardware accelerators, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is strong, placing in the top 25%, while Geekbench single-core and text processing scores fall into the average range. Conversely, multi-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores all ranking in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large-block reads and writes, but small-block cached reads are weak. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor, falling into the bottom 10%.

Given its balanced resource allocation and dedicated CPU cores, this instance offers a standard entry-level profile for general-purpose workloads. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for applications that require dedicated CPU resources without the overhead of high-density memory or local storage. However, the weak multi-core and database performance metrics suggest it is less optimal for heavy database operations or parallelized computing tasks. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded applications, and basic text processing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-4 is a general-purpose server configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware features 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, a 32 MB L3 cache, and no bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark evaluations show strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% on stress-ng, though multi-core and database operations fall into the bottom 25%. Large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression tasks perform at average levels. Lacking local storage and public IPv4 addresses, this instance represents a standard, dedicated resource tier. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads, single-threaded processing, lightweight web serving, and basic text processing tasks where high multi-core density is not required.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-4 is a general-purpose server featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance, suitable for lightweight web serving.

tagline: A dedicated general-purpose instance featuring balanced memory allocation and strong single-core processing capabilities for standard workloads.
